from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of Triples about an astronaut (or related entities) into a
    fluent, natural‑language description.  The implementation uses richer
    templates, handles direction‑reversed predicates (e.g. backupPilot where the
    object is the person and the subject is the mission), and builds a short
    paragraph by joining clauses with periods instead of a single comma‑list.
    """
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # Helper to strip surrounding quotes from literal strings.
    def clean(val: str) -> str:
        return val.strip('\"')

    # Templates for predicates where the subject is the astronaut.
    astronaut_templates = {
        "almaMater": lambda s, o: f"{s} studied at {clean(o)}",
        "birthDate": lambda s, o: f"{s} was born on {clean(o)}",
        "birthName": lambda s, o: f"{s} was originally named {clean(o)}",
        "birthPlace": lambda s, o: f"{s} was born in {clean(o)}",
        "dateOfRetirement": lambda s, o: f"{s} retired in {clean(o)}",
        "occupation": lambda s, o: f"{s} was a {clean(o)}",
        "status": lambda s, o: f"{s} is {clean(o).lower()}",
        "timeInSpace": lambda s, o: f"{s} spent {clean(o)} minutes in space",
        "mission": lambda s, o: f"{s} was a crew member of {clean(o)}",
        "selectedByNasa": lambda s, o: f"{s} was selected by NASA in {clean(o)}",
        "deathDate": lambda s, o: f"{s} died on {clean(o)}",
        "nationality": lambda s, o: (
            f"{s} is an American"
            if clean(o) in ("United States", "USA", "U.S.", "U.S.A.")
            else f"{s} is a {clean(o)} citizen"
        ),
        "servedAsChiefOfTheAstronautOfficeIn": lambda s, o: f"{s} served as chief of the Astronaut Office in {clean(o)}",
        "title": lambda s, o: f"{s} held the title \"{clean(o)}\"",
        "ribbonAward": lambda s, o: f"{s} received the {clean(o)} ribbon",
        "award": lambda s, o: f"{s} received the award {clean(o)}",
        "awards": lambda s, o: f"{s} has {clean(o)} awards",
        "deathPlace": lambda s, o: f"{s} died in {clean(o)}",
    }

    # Generic templates for non‑astronaut predicates that describe a relationship
    # where the object is the notable item (e.g., state bird, mascot, gemstone).
    # Helper for partsType predicate to produce a natural phrase.
    def _parts_type_phrase(subject: str, obj: str) -> str:
        obj_clean = clean(obj)
        # Specific handling for the common pattern "List of counties in X"
        if obj_clean.lower().startswith("list of counties in"):
            region = obj_clean.split("in", 1)[1].strip()
            return f"{subject} is a county in {region}"
        # Fallback: just describe the type.
        return f"{subject} is {obj_clean.lower()}"

    generic_templates = {
        "bird": lambda s, o, p="bird": f"The {clean(o).lower()} is the {p} of {clean(s)}",
        "mascot": lambda s, o, p="mascot": f"The {clean(o).lower()} is the {p} of {clean(s)}",
        "gemstone": lambda s, o, p="gemstone": f"The {clean(o).lower()} is the {p} of {clean(s)}",
        "fossil": lambda s, o, p="fossil": f"The {clean(o).lower()} is the {p} of {clean(s)}",
        "leader": lambda s, o, p="leader": f"{clean(o)} is the {p} of {clean(s)}",
        "president": lambda s, o, p="president": f"{clean(o)} is the {p} of {clean(s)}",
        "representative": lambda s, o, p="representative": f"{clean(o)} is the {p} of {clean(s)}",
        "award": lambda s, o, p="award": f"{clean(s)} received the {clean(o)}",
        "ribbonAward": lambda s, o, p="ribbon award": f"{clean(s)} received the {clean(o)}",
        # New predicate handling
        "senators": lambda s, o: f"{clean(o)} is a senator from {clean(s)}",
        "partsType": lambda s, o: _parts_type_phrase(s, o),
    }

    # Templates for predicates where the subject is a mission (or other entity)
    # and the object is a person or organization.
    mission_templates = {
        "operator": lambda s, o: f"{clean(s)} was operated by {clean(o)}",
        "backupPilot": lambda s, o: f"{clean(o)} was a backup pilot for {clean(s)}",
        "commander": lambda s, o: f"{clean(o)} was the commander of {clean(s)}",
        "crewMembers": lambda s, o: f"{clean(o)} was a crew member of {clean(s)}",
        "representative": lambda s, o: f"{clean(o)} was the representative of {clean(s)}",
    }

    # Gather information per astronaut and per mission.
    astronaut_info = {}
    mission_info = {}
    for triple in triples:
        s, p, o = triple.subject, triple.predicate, triple.object
        # Astronaut‑centric predicates
        if p in astronaut_templates:
            astronaut_info.setdefault(s, {"attrs": [], "missions": set()})
            astronaut_info[s]["attrs"].append(astronaut_templates[p](s, o))
        elif p == "mission":
            # link astronaut to mission
            astronaut_info.setdefault(s, {"attrs": [], "missions": set()})
            astronaut_info[s]["missions"].add(o)
            mission_info.setdefault(o, {"operator": None, "commander": None,
                                        "backupPilot": None, "crew": set()})
            mission_info[o]["crew"].add(s)
        # Mission‑centric predicates
        elif p in mission_templates:
            mission_info.setdefault(s, {"operator": None, "commander": None,
                                        "backupPilot": None, "crew": set()})
            if p == "operator":
                mission_info[s]["operator"] = o
            elif p == "commander":
                mission_info[s]["commander"] = o
            elif p == "backupPilot":
                mission_info[s]["backupPilot"] = o
            elif p == "crewMembers":
                mission_info[s]["crew"].add(o)
        # Generic non‑astronaut predicates (ignore for now)
        else:
            pass

    # Build a single sentence per astronaut, merging mission details.
    sentences = []
    for astronaut, data in astronaut_info.items():
        parts = [astronaut]
        # nationality already handled inside astronaut_templates
        if data["attrs"]:
            # remove duplicate subject mentions from attribute strings
            cleaned_attrs = [seg.replace(f"{astronaut} ", "").strip()
                             for seg in data["attrs"]]
            parts.append(", ".join(cleaned_attrs))
        # handle missions
        for mission in data["missions"]:
            mission_details = mission_info.get(mission, {})
            crew_others = mission_details.get("crew", set()) - {astronaut}
            crew_phrase = ""
            if crew_others:
                crew_list = list(crew_others)
                if len(crew_list) == 1:
                    crew_phrase = f" with {crew_list[0]} as a fellow crew member"
                else:
                    *first, last = crew_list
                    crew_phrase = f" with {', '.join(first)} and {last} as fellow crew members"
            extra = []
            if mission_details.get("operator"):
                extra.append(f"operated by {mission_details['operator']}")
            if mission_details.get("commander"):
                extra.append(f"commanded by {mission_details['commander']}")
            if mission_details.get("backupPilot"):
                extra.append(f"backup pilot {mission_details['backupPilot']}")
            extra_phrase = ""
            if extra:
                extra_phrase = " (" + ", ".join(extra) + ")"
            parts.append(f"was a crew member of {mission}{extra_phrase}{crew_phrase}")
        sentence = " ".join(parts) + "."
        sentences.append(sentence)

    paragraph = " ".join(sentences)
    return paragraph[0].upper() + paragraph[1:]

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
